behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at module boundaries: manifest parsing, dependency graph resolution (including transitive deps), and handoff to the vulnerability-check input set. A single-function unit test would miss wiring/normalization gaps.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against a fixture project with known direct and transitive dependencies (including nested/transitive-only packages) and assert the final query input set contains every expected package@version exactly once.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: The critical failure boundary is the real API contract/behavior: request encoding of version constraints and server-side matching. A mock can easily hide real mismatches where name-only matches are returned.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability DB endpoint (or official staging), query a dependency with multiple known vulnerable/non-vulnerable version ranges and verify only CVEs applicable to the manifest’s exact range are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic inside one boundary (comparator/sort function) with no required I/O or cross-module coupling to expose the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted in-memory CVE list covering all severities and same-severity CVSS ties; assert sorted order is severity rank first, then descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in parser/editor/serializer interaction (and often file persistence), where unrelated keys/formatting/order can be accidentally altered. Unit logic alone is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Apply upgrade on a real manifest fixture containing many deps and metadata; write result to disk and diff against baseline to assert only the targeted dependency version token changed.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real external API/process boundaries (git hosting service auth, branch/push/PR creation semantics). In-process mocks can pass while real PR creation or diff rendering fails.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner in a test repository on the real hosting provider and verify a PR is opened, its files-changed view shows exactly one dependency version change, and PR metadata targets the expected base/head branches.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver breaking-change detection is deterministic algorithmic logic at a single function/object boundary; no I/O is required to trigger the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Table-test semver pairs (including edge cases like pre-release/build metadata) and assert detector flags breaking change iff target major > current major under project semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is decision orchestration across semver classifier, PR-creation gate, and output/report module. The failure is at module interaction boundaries, not external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner flow with a fixture vulnerability requiring a major bump; assert PR client is not invoked and scanner report/output contains the expected review-required flag and reason.
